text: Block periodization with concentrated high-intensity intervals (5 HIT sessions in week 1, then 1 HIT/week for 3 weeks) produces superior VO2max gains compared to traditional distribution (2 HIT sessions/week) over 4 weeks, despite equal total training volume and intensity.
Block periodization of high-intensity work yields a large effect size for VO2max improvement compared to traditional even distribution of the same work.
Block periodization improves peak power output while traditional distribution of the same training volume does not.
Block periodization improves lactate threshold power (power at 2 mmol/L lactate) more than traditional distribution of the same training.

Concentrating high-intensity sessions into a single block week followed by low-intensity recovery weeks is more effective than spreading the same number of high-intensity sessions evenly across a mesocycle.
specific_value: 5 HIT sessions in 1 week + 3 weeks LIT vs 2 HIT sessions/week for 4 weeks
